moa: [99mTc]Tc-FAPi is a 99mTc-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor that targets FAP for SPECT diagnostic imaging to facilitate the staging and assessment of various solid tumors, including lung, pancreatic, and breast cancers.

[68Ga]Ga-FAPI-02 is a 68Ga-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor that targets FAP for PET/CT diagnostic imaging, widely utilized for early staging and metastasis evaluation of various solid tumors, such as gastrointestinal, lung, and breast cancers.

[177Lu]Lu-EB-FAPI (177Lu-LNC1004) is a 177Lu-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor modified with an Evans Blue (EB) moiety, that targets FAP for targeted radionuclide therapy of various advanced solid tumors, including thyroid and nasopharyngeal cancers.

[90Y]Y-FAPI-46 is a 90Y-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor that targets FAP to deliver high-energy Beta (β-) particles for targeted radionuclide therapy, primarily used for the internal radiation ablation of sarcomas, pancreatic cancer, and advanced metastatic solid tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-FAP6-IP-DOTA is a 177Lu-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor integrated with an albumin-binding lipophilic (IP) moiety, that targets FAP for targeted radionuclide therapy using Beta (β-) particles, primarily indicated for advanced solid tumors such as head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, lung, and gastrointestinal cancers.

[177Lu]Lu-FAP6-DOTA is a therapeutic radioligand labeled with the beta-emitting radionuclide lutetium-177 (¹⁷⁷Lu) that targets fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for targeted radiotherapy of FAP-positive solid tumors, exerting its anti-tumor effect by specifically binding to FAP-overexpressing cancer-associated fibroblasts in the tumor microenvironment and delivering localized radiation to kill tumor cells。

[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-4P-FAPI is a 177Lu-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibito modified with a PEG4 (4P) linker, that targets FAP for Beta (β-) radionuclide therapy, primarily indicated for the debulking and palliative treatment of various advanced metastatic solid tumors, such as pancreatic cancer and sarcomas.

[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-2P(FAPI)2 is a 177Lu-labeled dimeric fibroblast activation protein inhibitor, complexed via the DOTA chelator, that targets FAP for Beta (β-) therapy; its dimeric structure and PEG2 (2P) linker significantly enhance tumor uptake and retention in solid tumors like gastric, esophageal, and sarcomas.

[99mTc]Tc-6-1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the 6-1 peptide labeled with the radionuclide Technetium-99m, which targets the FAP overexpressed on tumor cells for the SPECT diagnosis and staging of malignant tumors

[99mTc]Tc-6-2 is a radiopharmaceutical comprising the 6-2 peptide labeled with the radionuclide Technetium-99m, which functions by specifically targeting FAP overexpressed in tumor angiogenesis for the SPECT diagnosis and imaging of solid tumors

[99mTc]Tc-6-3 is a radiopharmaceutical featuring the 6-3 peptide labeled with the radionuclide Technetium-99m, which acts by specifically binding to the FAP target on tumor cells or neovascular endothelial cells for the SPECT diagnosis and treatment response monitoring of solid tumors

[99mTc]Tc-6-4 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the 6-4 peptide labeled with the radionuclide Technetium-99m, which acts by specifically binding to the FAP target on tumor and neovascular surfaces for the SPECT diagnosis and molecular staging of malignant solid tumors

[99mTc]Tc-6-5 is a radiopharmaceutical comprising the 6-5 peptide labeled with the radionuclide Technetium-99m, which functions by specifically binding to the FAP overexpressed in tumor tissues for the SPECT diagnosis and precision imaging of various malignant tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-6-6 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the 6-6 peptide labeled with the radionuclide Technetium-99m, which acts by specifically targeting the FAP overexpressed in tumor lesions for the SPECT diagnosis, angiogenesis assessment, and post-operative monitoring of malignant solid tumors.

[99mTc][Tc-(CN-C5FAPI)6]+ is a 99mTc-labeled hexacoordinated cationic fibroblast activation protein inhibitor, complexed via isocyanide groups, that targets FAP for SPECT diagnostic imaging, primarily used for the lesion detection and staging of solid tumors such as breast, colorectal, and lung cancers.

[99mTc][Tc-(CN-PEG4-FAPI)6]+ is a 99mTc-labeled hexacoordinated cationic fibroblast activation protein inhibitor, complexed via isocyanide groups with a PEG4 linker, that targets FAP for SPECT diagnostic imaging of solid tumors like breast, lung, and liver cancers, utilizing PEGylation to optimize pharmacokinetics and improve image contrast.

[99mTc]Tc-TPPTS-HF2 is a 99mTc-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor derivative, that targets FAP for SPECT diagnostic imaging, primarily indicated for the non-invasive detection of solid tumors such as breast, non-small cell lung, and gastrointestinal cancers, offering high hydrophilicity and rapid blood clearance.

[99mTc]Tc-EDDA-HF2 is a 99mTc-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor, that targets FAP for SPECT diagnostic imaging, primarily utilized for the precise staging and lesion detection of solid tumors such as breast, lung, and nasopharyngeal cancers.

[99mTc]Tc-TPPMS-HF2 is a 99mTc-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor, that targets FAP for SPECT diagnostic imaging, primarily used for lesion identification in solid tumors like breast, lung, and head and neck cancers while optimizing biodistribution and contrast via ligand tuning.

[68Ga]Ga-Alb-FAPtp-01 is a 68Ga-labeled fibroblast activation protein inhibitor,conjugated with an Evans Blue derivative (Alb) for albumin binding, that targets FAP for PET/CT diagnostic imaging of solid tumors like liver and lung cancers, utilizing its albumin-binding property to prolong blood circulation and enhance tumor uptake.

[68Ga]Ga-FAPtp is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of FAPtp labeled with Gallium-68, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the PET diagnosis of various solid tumors such as pancreatic or gastric cancer.

[111In]In-FAP6-19 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of FAP6-19 labeled with Indium-111, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the SPECT diagnosis of various solid tumors like pancreatic or lung cancer.
[99mTc]Tc-L1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of L1 labeled with Technetium-99m, targeting FAP receptors overexpressed in tissues for the SPECT diagnosis of associated malignant tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-L2 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of L2 labeled with Technetium-99m, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the SPECT diagnosis of solid tumors such as gastrointestinal or lung cancer.
[111In]In-QCP01 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of QCP01 labeled with Indium-111, targeting gastrin-releasing peptide receptor (GRPR) for the SPECT diagnosis of prostate or breast cancer.
[111In]In-QCP02 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of QCP02 labeled with Indium-111, targeting gastrin-releasing peptide receptor (GRPR) for the SPECT diagnosis of prostate or breast cancer.

Al[18F]F-NSF is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of NSF labeled with Fluorine-18 via Al-F chelation, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the PET diagnosis of solid tumors such as gastric or lung cancer.

Al[18F]F-NSFB is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of NSFB labeled with Fluorine-18 via Al-F chelation, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the PET diagnosis of various solid tumors such as pancreatic or gastric cancer.

Al[18F]F-NSFBP2 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of NSFBP2 labeled with Fluorine-18 via Al-F chelation, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the PET diagnosis of solid tumors such as gastric or esophageal cancer.

Al[18F]F-NSFBP4 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of NSFBP4 labeled with Fluorine-18 via Al-F chelation, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) with high affinity for the PET diagnosis of various solid tumors (e.g., gastric or liver cancer).

[68Ga]Ga-FSDD0I is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of FSDD0I labeled with Gallium-68, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the PET diagnosis of solid tumors such as pancreatic or gastric cancer.

[177Lu]Lu-FSDD0I is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of FSDD0I labeled with Lutetium-177, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the radionuclide therapy of various solid tumors such as pancreatic cancer or sarcoma.

[68Ga]Ga-FSDD3I is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of FSDD3I labeled with Gallium-68, targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) for the PET diagnosis of solid tumors such as pancreatic or lung cancer.
[68Ga]Ga-AV02053 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors.
[68Ga]Ga-AV02070 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of prostate cancer.

[68Ga]Ga-FAPI-FUSCC-Ⅰ is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga using the DOTA chelator to specifically target Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and metastatic lesions.

[68Ga]Ga-FAPI-FUSCC-II is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) in a dimeric form, offering higher tumor uptake and retention for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors.
[68Ga]Ga-SB03178 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and metastases.

[68Ga]Ga-SB03045 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging in the staging, recurrence detection, and therapy monitoring of prostate cancer.

[68Ga]Ga-SB03058 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Somatostatin Receptors (SSTR) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging in the localization, staging, and treatment monitoring of neuroendocrine tumors (NETs).

[68Ga]Ga-AV01017 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of early diagnosis, staging, and biochemical recurrence in patients with prostate cancer.

[68Ga]Ga-AV01030 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of primary tumor assessment, nodal staging, and biochemical recurrence in patients with prostate cancer.

[68Ga]Ga-AV01038 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of precise staging, recurrence detection, and pre-therapeutic screening in patients with prostate cancer.

[68Ga]Ga-HTK03041 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their microenvironments.

[68Ga]Ga-FAPI-07 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their stromal microenvironments.

[86Y]Y-FAPI-07 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 86Y that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their stromal microenvironments, often used for dosimetry in theranostics.

[177Lu]Lu-FAPI-07 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 177Lu that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the targeted radionuclide therapy of various advanced or metastatic solid tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-FAPI-06 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and metastatic lesions.

[86Y]Y-FAPI-06 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 86Y that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors, frequently used for pharmacokinetic evaluation and dosimetry of its 90Y-labeled therapeutic counterparts.

[177Lu]Lu-FAPI-06 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 177Lu that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the targeted radionuclide therapy of various advanced solid tumors and their stromal components.

Al[18F]F-DD-FAPI is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 18F via the aluminum-fluoride (AlF) complexation method that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their stromal microenvironments.

Al[18F]F-PD-FAPI is a dimeric radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 18F via the aluminum-fluoride (AlF) complexation method that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their microenvironments.

[64Cu]Cu-FP-L1 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 64Cu that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their stromal microenvironments.

[99mTc]Tc-FL-L3 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 99mTc that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the SPECT/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and metastatic lesions.

[177Lu]Lu-ND-bisFAPI is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 177Lu that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) using a dimeric (bis-FAPI) structure, primarily used for the targeted radionuclide therapy (TRT) of various advanced solid tumors and their microenvironments.

[68Ga]Ga-GP01 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 68Ga that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the PET/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their stromal microenvironments.

[111In]In-3BP-3940 is a radiopharmaceutical labeled with the radionuclide 111In that targets Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP) for the SPECT/CT diagnostic imaging of various solid tumors and their stromal microenvironments.

[177Lu]Lu-3BP-3940 is a therapeutic radiopharmaceutical targeting the neurotensin receptor 1 (NTSR1), consisting of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (Lu-177) a small-molecule antagonist, indicated for the targeted radionuclide therapy (TRT) of NTSR1-positive solid tumors (e.g., pancreatic adenocarcinoma).

[111In]In-FAPI-46-I2 is a diagnostic radiopharmaceutical targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP), consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (In-111) complexed with a di-iodinated FAPI-derivative ligand, indicated for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of various FAP-expressing solid tumors.

[111In]In-FAPI-46-I is a diagnostic radiopharmaceutical targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP), consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (In-111) complexed with a mono-iodinated FAPI-46 derivative, indicated for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of various FAP-expressing solid tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-FUSCC-Tri is a diagnostic radiopharmaceutical targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP), consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (Ga-68) complexed with a trimeric FAPI-derivative ligand, indicated for the PET/CT imaging diagnosis and staging of various FAP-expressing solid tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-FAPI-FUSCC-Tri is a therapeutic radiopharmaceutical targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP), consisting of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (Lu-177) complexed with a trimeric FAPI-derivative ligand, indicated for the targeted radionuclide therapy (TRT) of various FAP-expressing solid tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-D0103 is a diagnostic radiopharmaceutical targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP), consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (Ga-68) complexed with the optimized D0103 (a FAPI-derivative) ligand, indicated for the PET/CT imaging diagnosis and tumor microenvironment evaluation of various FAP-expressing solid tumors.
radiochemical_method_description: 68Ga-radiolabeling was done by mixing a solution of sodium acetate (30 µL, 155 mg/mL in water) and 300 µL of generator eluate (80–85 MBq) with either 10 µg (for in vivo imaging) or 25 µg (for ex vivo biodistribution studies) of D0103 or Trivehexin. The mixture was incubated at 95°C for 15 min. Sodium acetate (100 µL) was then added to raise the pH to ~ 6.

moa: [68Ga]Ga-Trivehexin is a PET radiotracer that specifically binds to the αvβ6-integrin receptor overexpressed on certain tumor and fibrotic cells, enabling targeted visualization and localization of these tissues via positron emission tomography.
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling for preclinical experiments and determination of n-octanol–PBS distribution coefficients (log D7.4) was done as described . Applying this protocol, 5 nmol (0.5 nmol for cellular assays in order to maximize molar activity) of TRAP(AvB6)3 or Trivehexin were labeled with 68 Ga at pH 2, resulting RCY > 95%.

moa: [90Y]Y-3PRGD2 is a targeted radiotherapeutic agent that binds specifically to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed on tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, delivering localized beta-radiation to induce DNA damage and inhibit tumor growth.
radiochemical_method_description: For 111In and 90Y radiolabeling, 74-370 MBq of111InCl3 or 90YCl3 was diluted in 300μL of 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer (pH 5.2) and added to the DOTA-peptide conjugates (5 μg ofpeptide per mCi of111InCl3 or 90YCl3).

moa: [111In]In-3PRGD2 is a SPECT radiotracer that specifically binds to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed on tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, enabling non-invasive tumor imaging, localization, and dosimetry assessment via gamma-ray detection

[111In]In-RGD4 is a SPECT radiotracer consisting of a tetrameric RGD peptide that specifically binds with high affinity to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed on tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, enabling non-invasive, targeted tumor imaging and angiogenesis assessment via gamma camera detectionNational Center for Biotechnology Information

[90Y]Y-RGD4 is a targeted radiotherapeutic peptide radioconjugate that selectively binds to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, delivering cytotoxic β‑radiation to induce DNA damage and suppress tumor growth and angiogenesis.

[99mTc]Tc-(RGD)6 is a SPECT radiotracer consisting of a hexameric RGD peptide that binds with high avidity to integrin αvβ₃ overexpressed on tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, enabling non-invasive, targeted tumor imaging and angiogenesis assessment via gamma-ray detection.

[99mTc]Tc-(RGD)4 is a SPECT radiotracer that specifically targets integrin αvβ3 overexpressed on tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, allowing non-invasive tumor imaging and evaluation of angiogenesis through gamma camera detection.

[211At]At-(RGD)4 is an alpha-emitting radiotherapeutic agent that selectively targets integrin αvβ3 overexpressed on tumor neovasculature and cancer cells, delivering high-linear-energy-transfer alpha radiation to induce lethal DNA damage and inhibit tumor growth

[68Ga]Ga-DOTA-5G is a PET radiopharmaceutical that targets integrin αvβ6, a receptor overexpressed in many epithelial cancers . It is used for non-invasive tumor imaging, staging, and theranostic planning
radiochemical_method_description: Gallium-68 was eluted from a 68Ge/68Ga generator ((Eckert and Ziegler, Berlin, Germany) using 5 mL of 0.1 N HCl, and 1.5 mL fraction containing the bulk radioactivity was used. Radiolabeling was conducted at pH 4.0 in 1M HEPES buffer.

moa: [68Ga]GaDOTA-ABM-5G is a PET radiotracer that specifically targets integrin αvβ6 overexpressed in various epithelial tumors for non-invasive tumor imaging and disease staging
radiochemical_method_description: Gallium-68 was eluted from a 68Ge/68Ga generator ((Eckert and Ziegler, Berlin, Germany) using 5 mL of 0.1 N HCl, and 1.5 mL fraction containing the bulk radioactivity was used. Radiolabeling was conducted at pH 4.0 in 1M HEPES buffer.

moa: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-5G is a targeted radiotherapeutic that selectively binds to integrin αvβ6 overexpressed on tumor cells, delivering cytotoxic beta radiation to induce DNA damage and suppress tumor growth
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling of peptides 1 and 2 with lutetium-177 (ITM (Garching, Germany) was conducted at pH 5.0 in 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer by adding 1 (44 μg) or 2 (46 μg) to [177Lu]LuCl3 (a 148 MBq aliquot of the commercial [177Lu]LuCl3 in 0.04 N HCl in 100-150 μL buffer) and ascorbic acid (25 μL of a 5 mg/mL aq. solution).

moa: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-ABM-5G is a targeted radiotherapeutic agent that specifically binds to integrin αvβ6 overexpressed in epithelial tumors, delivering cytotoxic beta-radiation to induce DNA damage and inhibit tumor proliferation
radiochemical_method_description: Radiolabeling of peptides 1 and 2 with lutetium-177 (ITM (Garching, Germany) was conducted at pH 5.0 in 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer by adding 1 (44 μg) or 2 (46 μg) to [177Lu]LuCl3 (a 148 MBq aliquot of the commercial [177Lu]LuCl3 in 0.04 N HCl in 100-150 μL buffer) and ascorbic acid (25 μL of a 5 mg/mL aq. solution).

moa: [68Ga]NODAGA-RGD is a PET radiotracer that binds specifically to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor neovasculature, enabling non-invasive imaging of angiogenesis and tumor localization
radiochemical_method_description: to 10 μl NODAGA-RGD (1 μg/μl in water) 60 μl 1.9 M sodium acetate solution and 300 μl 68GaCl3 of the main fraction of the generator eluate (approx. 100–200 MBq in 0.1 M HCl) were added. The solution was allowed to react for 15 min at room temperature.

moa: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-RGD is a PET radiotracer that specifically targets integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor neovasculature, allowing non-invasive imaging of tumor angiogenesis and tumor localization
radiochemical_method_description: to 10 μl NODAGA-RGD (1 μg/μl in water) 60 μl 1.9 M sodium acetate solution and 300 μl 68GaCl3 of the main fraction of the generator eluate (approx. 100–200 MBq in 0.1 M HCl) were added. The solution was allowed to react for 15 min at room temperature.

moa: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-E{E[c(RGDyK)]2}2 is a multimeric RGD-based PET radiotracer that binds with high affinity to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor vasculature, enabling non-invasive imaging of tumor angiogenesis and tumor characterization
radiochemical_method_description:
In brief, 20 mLof 64CuCl2 (74 MBq in 0.1N HCl) were diluted in 400 mL of 0.1 mol/L sodium acetate buffer (pH 6.5) and added to the DOTA-RGD multimer (a 1 mg/mL
solution of peptide was made and separated into aliquots; 5 mgof DOTA-RGD tetramer and 10 mg of DOTA-RGD octamer per 37 MBq of 64Cu were used for the labeling). The reaction mixture was incubated for 1 h at 50 C.

moa: [64Cu]Cu-DOTA-E(E{E[c(RGDyK)]2}2)2 is a multimeric RGD-conjugated PET radiotracer that targets integrin αvβ3 in tumor neovasculature for non-invasive imaging of angiogenesis and tumor progression
[68Ga]Ga-NGR-RGD is a dual-targeting PET radiotracer that binds to both CD13 and integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor neovasculature, enabling enhanced tumor imaging and angiogenesis evaluation
radiochemical_method_description: In brief, 2 μL of NGR-RGD, NOTA-c(RGDyK) or NOTA-c(KNGRE) (2 mM) was added to 150 μL buffer (0.25 M sodium acetate, pH 6.8). Subsequently, 500 μL [68Ga]GaCl3 in 0.05 M HCl (180–400 MBq) was added and mixed. The final pH of the reaction mixture was approximately 4.0. Then, the reaction mixture was heated to 90°C and reacted for 5 min. After cooling to room temperature, 68Ga-NGR-RGD, 68Ga-NOTA-c(RGDyK) (denoted as 68Ga-RGD), and 68Ga-NOTA-c(KNGRE) (denoted as 68Ga-NGR) were obtained and used for the subsequent studies without

moa: [68Ga]Ga-sCD146 is a PET radiotracer that targets angiomotin (AMOT) by binding to its ligand CD146, enabling non-invasive imaging of angiogenesis and tumor neovascularization
radiochemical_method_description: gallium-68 chloride (68GaCl3, 200.7 ± 41 MBq/500 µL) was buffered with a fresh 2M ammonium acetate solution (pH 7.4) and 10 µg of RGD2 precursor (catalogue number 9804, purchased at ABX, Radeberg, Germany) was added. The reaction mixture was stirred at room temperature for 15 min.

radiochemical_method_description: Gallium chloride  was obtained from the elution of a commercial TiO2-based68Ge/68Ga generator (Galliapharm, Eckert-Ziegler) using 0.1 N HCl, and buffered with a fresh 4 M ammonium acetate solution (pH 7.4) and added to sCD146 precursor (15 渭g), reaching a final pH of 6.0. The reaction mixture was stirred at room temperature for 15 min with gentle agitation every 5 min.

moa: [99mTc]Tc-(HYNIC-tetramer)(tricine)(TPPTS) is a SPECT radiotracer that targets integrin αvβ3 in tumor neovascu­lature for non-invasive imaging of tumor angiogenesis and tumor localization.it is excreted via both renal and hepatobiliary routes
radiochemical_method_description: To a vial filled with a 1.0 mL solution (pH ) 4.8) containing 5 mg of TPPTS, 6.5 mg of tricine, 40 mg of mannitol, 38.5 mg of disodium succinate hexahydrate, and 12.7 mg of succinic acid were added 0.2 mL of HYNIC-tetramer solution (100 µg/ mL in water) and 0.3 mL of Na[99mTcO4] solution (370-1850 MBq/mL). The vial was heated at 100 °C for 20-25 min in a lead-shielded water bath. After heating, the vial was placed back into the lead pig and allowed to stand at room temperature for ∼10 min.

moa: [67Ga]Ga-CD6 is a SPECT radiotracer that selectively targets integrin αvβ6 overexpressed in various tumors for non-invasive tumor imaging with minimized nonspecific uptake in normal organs
radiochemical_method_description: The purified NOTA-peptides (20-40 µg) were incubated with 67GaCl3 or 67Ga-citrate (2-15 MBq) in 0.2 M sodium acetate buffer (100-300 µL, pH 4.0) at 80ºC for 15 minutes. The reaction solution was then purified by analytical RP-HPLC.

moa: [68Ga]Ga-NOTA-RGD-BBN is a dual-targeting PET radiotracer that binds to both integrin αvβ3 and gastrin-releasing peptide receptor (GRPR) overexpressed in tumors, allowing improved non-invasive tumor imaging and angiogenesis evaluation
radiochemical_method_description: The 68Ga labeling was performed according to methods previously described [3]. Briefly, 10 nmol of NOTA-RGD, NOTA-BBN or NOTA-RGD-BBN peptide was dissolved in 500 μl of 0.1 M sodium acetate buffer and incubated with 185 MBq of 68Ga for 10 min at 40°C

[99mTc]Tc-O(MAG2-3G3-dimer) is a SPECT radiotracer that selectively binds to integrin αvβ6 overexpressed in tumor cells for non-invasive tumor imaging and detection.It was only 45–55% from the homogenates of tumor, kidney, and liver tissues.
radiochemical_method_description: To a lyophilized vial containing 2.28 mg of NaH2PO4, 11.5 mg of Na2HPO4,50mgof α-D-glucoheptonic acid, 50 μgofSnCl2·2H2O, and25µlofthe MAG2-3G3-dimer conjugate was added 1.0 ml of Na99mTcO4 solution (10–50 mCi/ml). The vial was heated at 100°C for 20– 25 min in a lead-shielded water bath. After heating, the vial was placed back into the lead pig and allowed to stand at room temperature for ∼10 min.

moa: [99mTc]Tc–HYNIC–cHK is a SPECT radiotracer that targets tumor-associated receptors for non-invasive tumor imaging and localization andradiotracer remained intact in urine but underwent metabolism in blood
radiochemical_method_description: For 99mTc labeling, a mixture of 20 mg tricine (100 mg/mL in 25 mmol/L succinate buffer, pH 5.0), 740 MBq (20 mCi) Na99mTcO4, and 30 ll SnCl2 (1 lg/lL in 0.1 mol/L HCl) was successively added to 20 lg of HYNIC–cHK with constant stirring at 99 °C for 10 min. Then 100 ll of ethylenediamine-N,N0-diacetic acid (100 mg/mL) was added to the mixture with contant stirring at 99 °C for 20 min. After allowing it to cool down to room temperature, the mixture was purified with Sep-Pak C18 cartridges (Waters) as preciously described

moa: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-R01-MG is a PET radiotracer that specifically targets integrin αvβ6 overexpressed in lung cancer and other epithelial tumors for non-invasive tumor imaging and evaluation.Renal excretion is the primary clearance pathway
radiochemical_method_description:
DOTA-
R01-MG was mixed with 68GaCl3 in sodium acetate buffer (pH
= 4.5) for 15 min at 90 °C. The mixture was purified by PD-10
column (GE Healthcare Life Sciences). Twelve 500 μL
samples were collected from the column. The active fraction
(usually 6 and 7) was then used for subsequent studies. The

moa: iRGD-C6-lys(211At-ATE)-C6-DA7R is a dual-targeting alpha-emitting radiotherapeutic agent that binds to integrin αvβ3 and neuropilin-1 overexpressed in tumor vasculature, delivering targeted alpha-particle radiation for cancer therapy.Renal excretion is the main clearance pathway
radiochemical_method_description: The labeling precursor (10 μL, 10 mg/mL in MeOH) was mixed with 211At (200 MBq, 50 μL) and N-iodosuccinimide (NIS, 5 μL, 3 mg/mL in MeOH with 1% acetic acid) in methanol. The mixture was then incubated at 37 °C for 10 min, and the labeling reaction was terminated by adding Na2S2O3 solution (5 μL, 20 mg/mL in water). The product was isolated by gel filtration on a SephadexTM G-10 column, with 0.1 mol L−1 phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.6) acting as the eluting solution. Quality control was performed using radio-TLC (thin layer chromatography), with acetone: water (v/v = 3:1) as the mobile phase (retention factor [Rf] iRGD-C6-lys(211At-ATE)-C6-DA7R = 0−0.1, [Rf] free 211At = 0.9−1). Radiolabeling of iRGD and DA7R using 211At was performed according to the same labeling method in the above experiment.

moa: [67Ga]Ga-CG6 is a SPECT radiotracer that specifically targets integrin αvβ6 overexpressed in various epithelial tumors for non-invasive tumor imaging and detection.

moa: [18F]F-RWY is a PET radiotracer that specifically targets integrin α6 overexpressed in hepatocellular carcinoma for non-invasive tumor imaging and detection.
radiochemical_method_description: Briefly, we used lyophilized kits for rapid radiofluorination of NOTA-RWY peptide. The kits consisted of 8 nmol of NOTA-RWY and 6 nmol of AlCl3•6H2O. moa: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-FAPI-RGD is a PET radiotracer that simultaneously targets fibroblast activation protein (FAP) and integrin αvβ3 for dual-modal imaging of tumor stroma and angiogenesis.
radiochemical_method_description: Chelation with gallium-68 was performed by incubating 4.0 mL generator eluate (0.05 M HCl, 0.74−0.99 GBq) with a mixture of 10 nmol DOTA-precursor and 1.0 mL NaOAc (0.25 M) at 100 ◦C for 10 min. The mixture was diluted with 2.0 mL water and passed through a C18 cartridge. Subsequently, the C18 cartridge was washed with 5 mL water and eluted with a mixture of ethanol/H2O (v/v, 1 mL) to obtain the desired product. As regards lutetium-177 labelling, 74 MBq [177Lu] LuCl3 were diluted with 0.16 mL NaOAc (0.4 M, pH 5.5) and added to the precursor (28.0 nmol) in gentisic acid (2.0 mg). The reaction mixture was incubated at 95 ◦C for 15 min, then, it was purified by HPLC before its use in the experiments (Supplementary Fig. S8).

moa: [177Lu]Lu-AB-3PRGD2 is a radiotherapeutic agent that targets integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor neovasculature and delivers cytotoxic beta radiation to treat various solid tumors.
radiochemical_method_description: No carrier-added 177LuCl3 for clinical use was purchased from ITM (Germany), with a specific activity of 3000 GBq/mg and a concentration of 36e44 GBq/mL. Typically, 3.7 GBq (100 mCi) 177LuCl3 was added to 200 mL of 0.5 mol/L NaOAc (pH 5.6), and the 177Lu dilution was injected into the vial with 100 mgof DOTAAB-3PRGD2 (Fig. 2A) provided by the Medical Isotopes Research Center, School of Basic Medical Sciences, Peking University. The mixture was heated in an upright position at 100 C for 30 min, then purified through a C18 cartridge and sterilized through a 0.22-mm aseptic filtration membrane.

moa: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-HEVNPs is a PET radiotracer that targets tumor neovasculature by binding to integrin αvβ3, enabling non-invasive imaging of angiogenesis and tumor progression.
radiochemical_method_description: HEVNPs (0.13 mg/ml in 100 µl in PB) were diluted with metal-free 0.25 M ammonium acetate buffer (0.8 ml) in a reaction tube (5-ml Protein LoBind microtube, Eppendorf). This was followed by adding freshly eluted [68Ga]GaCl3 in 0.1 M HCl (80–140 MBq in 1 ml). The final reaction volume was 1.9 ml, and the reaction pH was 4.5. The reaction solution was mixed for 30 min at 63◦C with 350 rpm.

moa: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-HEVNP-LXY30 is a dual-targeted PET radiotracer that binds to both tumor neovasculature and specific tumor-associated receptors for enhanced non-invasive imaging of solid tumors.
radiochemical_method_description: [68Ga]Ga-DOTA-HEVNP-LXY30 (700 µl in PB; 1.8-2.2 MBq) were added into 1.5-ml Protein LoBind microtubes containing 700 µl of PBS (0.01 M, pH 7.4), 100% human plasma, iron (FeCl3⋅ 6 H2O, 18 mM in PBS), or CO2-independent cell medium (from Gibco). All the samples (n = 3 per testing solution) were incubated at 37◦C. The amount of the intact 68Ga-radiolabeled HEVNP-LXY30 was monitored by radio-TLC at 5 min, 15 min, 30 min, and 60 min after the initiation of the incubation with the testing solutions.

moa: [177Lu]Lu-DOTA-2.5D is a radiotherapeutic that selectively binds to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed on tumor cells and neovasculature, delivering cytotoxic beta-radiation to induce DNA damage and inhibit angiogenesis in solid tumors.>90% intact in urine at 1 h p.i.

moa: Al[18F]F-NOTA-Rd2 is a PET radiotracer that specifically binds to integrin αvβ3 overexpressed in tumor neovasculature for non-invasive imaging of tumors and angiogenesis.Primarily renal excretion

moa: Al[18F]F-NOTA-A6P is a PET radiotracer that selectively targets integrin α6 overexpressed in tumor cells for non-invasive tumor imaging. Primarily renal excretion

The cartridge was then washed with water (20 mL) and eluted with 1.5 mL of 70 % ethanol, yielding the final product.

moa: [99mTc]Tc-Keto-RGD-N4 achieves targeted SPECT imaging of tumors by specifically binding to integrin αvβ3 receptors highly expressed on tumor neovasculature and tumor cells via its RGD sequence, with 99mTc chelated by the N4 ligand.Primarily renal excretion

The reaction mixture was reached to 1 mL by normal saline and incubated at room temperature for 10 min. 200 μL of the peptide aqueous solution (0.1 μg/ μL) was added to the radiolabeled mixture and incubated at 50 °C for 30 min.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKD-PG-TPPTS is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated consisting of a PG linker and TPPTS to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer, utilizing emitted gamma rays to achieve precise localization of tumor lesions.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKD-PG-EDDA is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated consisting of a PG linker and EDDA to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer, utilizing emitted gamma rays to achieve precise localization and imaging evaluation of tumor lesions.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKD-PEG-TPPMS is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated consisting of a PEG linker and TPPMS to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKD-PEG-TPPMS is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated system consisting of a PEG linker and TPPMS to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKD-PEG-EDDA is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated system consisting of a PEG linker and EDDA to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKT-TPPTS is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKT-TPPMS is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[99mTc]Tc-EUKT-EDDA is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the SPECT/CT imaging diagnosis of prostate cancer.The main metabolic organs are the kidneys.

[177Lu]Lu-PSMA-TB-01 is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) conjugated to a ligand to target Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the radionuclide therapy of prostate cancer.The kidneys are the main excretory organs.

[90Y]Y-DOTA-4D5 is a radioimmunotherapeutic agent composed of the radionuclide Yttrium-90 (90Y) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody 4D5 (a precursor of Trastuzumab) to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the radioimmunotherapy of HER2-positive tumors (such as breast cancer).

[111In]In-DTPA-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-trastuzumab Fab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto the Fab fragment of the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[213Bi]Bi-CHX-A''-Herceptin is a radioimmunotherapeutic agent composed of the radionuclide Bismuth-213 (213Bi) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab (Herceptin) to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the alpha-particle radioimmunotherapy of HER2-positive tumors.

drug_synonyms: Technetium-99m labeled trastuzumab Fab fragments；⁹⁹ᵐTc-trastuzumab Fab；⁹⁹ᵐTc-HYNIC-anti-HER2 Fab
moa: [99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-trastuzumab Fab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated to the Fab fragment of the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[212Pb]Pb-TCMC-Herceptin is a radioimmunotherapeutic agent composed of the radionuclide Lead-212 (212Pb) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab (Herceptin) to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the alpha-particle radioimmunotherapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[86Y]Y-IB4M-Herceptin is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Yttrium-86 (86Y) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab (Herceptin) to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for PET imaging diagnosis and pharmacokinetic dosimetry assessment of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-CHX-A''-DTPA-ABD-(ZHER2:342)2 is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) conjugated to a fusion protein. This protein consists of an Albumin-Binding Domain (ABD) and two Affibody molecules (ZHER2:342) targeting Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2), used for the radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-MAG3-ZHER2:342 is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated to the ZHER2:342 Affibody molecule to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-CGG-ZHER2:342 is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated via a CGG (Cys-Gly-Gly) peptide-based chelating sequence to the ZHER2:342 Affibody molecule to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-CGGG-ZHER2:342 is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) conjugated via a CGGG (Cys-Gly-Gly-Gly) peptide-based chelating sequence to the ZHER2:342 Affibody molecule to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-NLS-trastuzumab is an indium-111 radiolabeled nuclear localization sequence-modified trastuzumab imaging agent, which specifically targets HER2 antigen and is applied for SPECT imaging diagnosis and targeted distribution evaluation of HER2-positive breast cancer and other malignant tumors.

[86Y]Y-Trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Yttrium-86 (86Y) labeled onto the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab to target Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors. It is often used for pharmacokinetic assessment as a surrogate for Yttrium-90 (90Y) therapy.

[99mTc]Tc-His₆-(ZHER2:4)₂ is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) chelated to a dimeric ZHER2:4 Affibody molecule. By specifically targeting Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2) and utilizing the His₆-tag for simplified labeling, it is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-Benzyl-DTPA-ZHER2:342 is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) conjugated to the ZHER2:342 Affibody molecule. By specifically targeting Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2), it is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[89Zr]Zr-DFO-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Zirconium-89 (89Zr) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes the long half-life of 89Zr to match the pharmacokinetics of the antibody, serving as a clinical gold standard for PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-NOTA-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes the high chelating stability of NOTA for copper ions, used for PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-PEG₁₂-rL-A9 is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) conjugated to rL-A9 (an affibody or ligand targeting PSMA). Utilizing the PEG₁₂ chain to improve pharmacokinetics, it specifically targets Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen (PSMA) for the radionuclide therapy of prostate cancer.

[177Lu]Lu-DTPA-F(ab′)₂-pertuzumab is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (¹⁷⁷Lu) conjugated to the F(ab′)₂ fragment of Pertuzumab. It specifically targets Domain II of HER2, utilizing the smaller size of the F(ab′)₂ fragment to achieve faster blood clearance and better tumor penetration for the radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

Trastuzumab-PEG6-DM1 is an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) consisting of the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab linked to the cytotoxic agent DM1 (a maytansinoid derivative) via a PEG6 spacer. By specifically targeting HER2 receptors, it delivers the potent microtubule inhibitor DM1 directly into tumor cells, primarily used for the targeted therapy of HER2-positive cancers.

Pertuzumab-PEG6-DM1 is an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) consisting of the monoclonal antibody Pertuzumab conjugated to the cytotoxic agent DM1 via a PEG6 linker. It specifically targets Domain II of HER2 (inhibiting receptor dimerization) to deliver the microtubule inhibitor DM1 into tumor cells, used in the targeted therapy research of HER2-positive cancers.

[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-trastuzumab is a radioimmunopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes the beta particles emitted by 177Lu to kill tumor cells through ionizing radiation, primarily used for radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive cancers, while also allowing for imaging monitoring via its gamma emissions.

[177Lu]Lu-ABY-027 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) labeled onto the second-generation Affibody molecule ABY-027. It targets HER2-positive tumors with high specificity. Utilizing the beta particles emitted by 177Lu for radionuclide therapy, its design leverages the small molecular size of the affibody to achieve superior tumor penetration and rapid clearance from non-target tissues.

[68Ga]Ga-DOTA-GGG-H6F is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto a targeting peptide. The molecule features a GGG (Gly-Gly-Gly) linker and the H6F peptide (typically targeting HER2). It specifically binds to targets on the tumor surface and is primarily used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors, leveraging its small molecular size to achieve rapid blood clearance and high-contrast imaging.

[99mTc]Tc-HYNIC-PEG12-rL-A9 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) labeled onto the rL-A9 peptide. The molecule incorporates PEG12 as a pharmacokinetic modifier to improve in vivo biodistribution. It specifically targets HER2, leveraging the significantly enhanced affinity and tumor uptake provided by the PEG12 linker, and is primarily used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-NOTA-MVK-ZHER2:2891 is a radiotracer consisting of Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto the ZHER2:2891 Affibody. The molecule incorporates an MVK (typically Met-Val-Lys) cleavable linker, designed to reduce renal radioactivity retention through the action of brush border enzymes. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor for high-quality PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-trastuzumab Fab is a radiotracer consisting of Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto the Fab fragment of Trastuzumab. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor. Compared to full antibodies, its smaller molecular weight allows for faster blood clearance and an earlier imaging time window, primarily used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-TMDI4-2 is a radioimmunoconjugate (RIC) consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2. The core design utilizes increased positive charge to accelerate the blood clearance of non-targeted antibodies, thereby significantly improving the tumor-to-blood ratio for the imaging research of HER2-positive tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-DOTA-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-15.5-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-NODAGA-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-HP2 is a radiopharmaceutical composed of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) complexed with the macrocyclic polyamine chelator HP2 (1,4,7,10-tetraazacyclododecane-1,7-diacetic acid). It typically functions as a bone-targeting agent, utilizing its high affinity for bone tissue for the radionuclide therapy of bone metastases.

[64Cu]Cu-PCB-TE2A-PEG4-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. The molecule incorporates a PEG4 linker to optimize pharmacokinetics. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-BTz-DA is a radioimmunopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor and is primarily used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-PTz-DA is a radioimmunopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto the monoclonal antibody Pertuzumab. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor (binding to Domain II) and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

Z-HP9:[¹⁷⁷Lu]Lu-HP16 is a bispecific or multi-component radioimmunoconjugate. It consists of the Z-HP9 Affibody targeting the HER2 receptor and the HP16 moiety labeled with the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (¹⁷⁷Lu). The agent utilizes the high targeting specificity of the affibody to deliver ¹⁷⁷Lu to the lesion for radionuclide therapy via beta particle emission, primarily used in the targeted therapy research of HER2-positive tumors.

Pb-214/Bi-214-TCMC-Trastuzumab is a radioimmunopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Lead-214 (Pb-214) and its daughter Bismuth-214 (Bi-214) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes emitted radiation (particularly in-situ generated alpha particles or high-energy beta particles) for radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive cancers.

Al[18F]F-NOTA-PBB is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of Fluorine-18 (18F) labeled via the AlF (Aluminum Fluoride) chelation method using the NOTA chelator onto a PBB (antibody fragment or peptide) derivative. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[225Ac]Ac-macropa-T-PEG₆-DM1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Actinium-225 (225Ac) labeled onto a T-DM1 (Trastuzumab emtansine) derivative. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor and is used for the radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[225Ac]Ac-Proteus-DOTA is a radioimmunopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Actinium-225 (225Ac) labeled onto Proteus (an engineered multivalent antibody platform or scaffold protein). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes emitted alpha particles for radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-DOTA-NLS-rL-A9 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) labeled. The molecule incorporates an NLS (Nuclear Localization Sequence), rL (linker), and A9 (a HER2-targeting affibody or peptide). It specifically targets HER2 receptors with intended nuclear localization for the radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[52Mn]Mn-Oxo-DO3A-trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Manganese-52 (52Mn) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

PEP40233 (HER2) is a specific peptide-based probe (typically based on the Affibody scaffold) targeting Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2). When labeled with radionuclides, it is indicated for the PET/CT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors, including breast and gastric cancers.

211At-anti-HER2 mAb (SA stabilized) is a radioimmunopharmaceutical consisting of Astatine-211 (211At) labeled onto a HER2-targeting monoclonal antibody. Utilizing SA (succinimidyl or related stabilizing groups) for structural stabilization, it specifically targets HER2 receptors for radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors via alpha particle emission.

[68Ga]Ga-DOTA-(Ser)₃-LTVSPWY is a radiopeptide tracer consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled. The molecule features a (Ser)₃ (tri-serine) linker and the specific peptide sequence LTVSPWY. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[89Zr]Zr-AAZTHAG-C5-Trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Zirconium-89 (89Zr) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-TP1580 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto a targeting moiety (typically a peptide or small molecule). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

Al[18F]F-RESCA-5F7 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of Fluorine-18 (18F) labeled via the AlF (Aluminum Fluoride) method onto 5F7 (a HER2-specific single-domain antibody or nanobody). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-DOTA-10H8 minibody is a radioimmunopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto the 10H8 minibody. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is primarily used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[89Zr]Zr-Trastuzumab is a radioimmunotracer composed of the radionuclide Zirconium-89 (89Zr) conjugated to the monoclonal antibody Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[89Zr]Zr-DFO-T-PEG₆-DM1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Zirconium-89 (89Zr) labeled onto a T-DM1 (Trastuzumab emtansine) derivative. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[225Ac]Ac-macropa-T-PEG₆-DM1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Actinium-225 (225Ac) labeled onto a T-DM1 (Trastuzumab emtansine) derivative. It specifically targets the HER2 receptor and is used for the radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-(ZHER2:2891)₂–ABD–MC-DM1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) labeled onto a conjugate comprising a bivalent HER2-targeting affibody (ZHER2:2891)₂, an albumin-binding domain (ABD), and the cytotoxic drug DM1. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-(ZHER2:2891)₂–ABD–E₃–MC-DM1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) labeled onto a conjugate comprising a bivalent HER2-targeting affibody (ZHER2:2891)₂, an albumin-binding domain (ABD), a negatively charged E₃ linker, and the cytotoxic drug DM1. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[99mTc]Tc-(ZHER2:2891)₂–ABD–E₆–MC-DM1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Technetium-99m (99mTc) labeled onto a conjugate comprising a bivalent HER2-targeting affibody (ZHER2:2891)₂, an albumin-binding domain (ABD), a negatively charged E₆ (hexa-glutamate) linker, and the cytotoxic drug DM1. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-NOTA-ZHER2:S1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) labeled onto the HER2-targeting affibody ZHER2:S1. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-NODAGA-ZHER2:S1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) labeled onto the HER2-targeting affibody ZHER2:S1. Compared to NOTA, NODAGA offers enhanced coordination stability. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-NODAGA-ZHER2:S1 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto the HER2-targeting affibody ZHER2:S1. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[211At]At-SAGMB-5F7 is a radioimmunotherapeutic consisting of the Alpha-emitting radionuclide Astatine-211 (211At) labeled onto 5F7 (a HER2-specific nanobody). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes high linear energy transfer (LET) α-particles from 211At for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) of HER2-positive tumors.

iso-[211At]At-SAGMB-5F7 is a radioimmunotherapeutic consisting of the Alpha-emitting radionuclide Astatine-211 (211At) labeled onto 5F7 (a HER2-specific nanobody). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and utilizes high linear energy transfer (LET) α-particles from 211At for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-CHX-A’’-DTPA-Trastuzumab is a radioimmunotherapeutic consisting of the radionuclide Lutetium-177 (177Lu) labeled onto Trastuzumab. It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the radionuclide therapy of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-NOTA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-NODAGA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-DOTA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-DOTAGA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-NOTA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-NODAGA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-DOTA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-(HE)₃DANS-ADAPT6-GSSC-DOTAGA is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto ADAPT6 (a scaffold protein-based HER2-targeting molecule) derivative. It incorporates an (HE)₃DANS tag, specifically targets HER2 receptors, and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-DOTA-Z HER2:2891 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto Z HER2:2891 (an Affibody molecule targeting HER2). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[111In]In-DOTAGA-Z HER2:2891 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Indium-111 (111In) labeled onto Z HER2:2891 (a HER2-targeting Affibody molecule). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the SPECT imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-DOTA-Z HER2:2891 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto Z HER2:2891 (a HER2-targeting Affibody molecule). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-DOTAGA-Z HER2:2891 is a radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Gallium-68 (68Ga) labeled onto Z HER2:2891 (a HER2-targeting Affibody molecule). It specifically targets HER2 receptors and is used for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-@Fe₃O₄-CEPA-trastuzumab is a targeted radiopharmaceutical consisting of the radionuclide Actinium-225 (225Ac) loaded onto CEPA-modified Fe₃O₄ magnetic nanoparticles conjugated with Trastuzumab, used for MRI-guided targeted alpha therapy (TAT) of HER2-positive tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-@Fe₃O₄-CEPA is a radioactive nanocomposite consisting of the radionuclide Actinium-225 (225Ac) loaded onto CEPA-modified Fe₃O₄ magnetic nanoparticles, used for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) or as an intermediate for constructing targeted radiopharmaceuticals.

[89Zr]Zr-DFO-trastuzumab is a radioimmunoconjugate consisting of the radionuclide Zirconium-89 (89Zr) labeled onto Trastuzumab, specifically targeting HER2 receptors for the PET imaging diagnosis of HER2-positive tumors.

[89Zr]Zr-DFO-IgG1 is a radioimmunoconjugate consisting of the radionuclide Zirconium-89 (89Zr) labeled onto a non-specific IgG1 immunoglobulin (often used as a control), utilized in PET imaging studies to evaluate non-specific accumulation or as a reference for targeted antibodies.

[64Cu]Cu-NOTA-Bn-SCN-2-methyl-2-phenethyl-2H-benzopyran is a radiotracer consisting of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) labeled onto a 2-methyl-2-phenethyl-2H-benzopyran derivative, used for PET imaging diagnosis of specific biological targets.

[64Cu]Cu-NOTA-Bn-SCN-2-methyl-2-phenethyl-2H-benzopyran is a radiotracer consisting of the radionuclide Copper-64 (64Cu) labeled onto a 2-methyl-2-phenethyl-2H-benzopyran derivative, typically used for PET imaging to monitor reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels or specific enzyme activities in the tumor microenvironment.

[64Cu]Cu-PSMA I&T is a radiotracer comprising Copper-64 conjugated to a PSMA-targeting small-molecule ligand, utilizing positron emission for the PET diagnostic imaging of prostate cancer staging, biochemical recurrence detection, and therapeutic response assessment.

[68Ga]Ga-JH03 is a radiotracer comprising Gallium-68 conjugated to a PSMA-targeting small-molecule ligand, utilizing positron emission for the PET diagnostic imaging of prostate cancer and its metastases.

[177Lu]Lu-JH04 is a radioligand therapy comprising Lutetium-177 conjugated  to a Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP)-targeting small-molecule inhibitor, utilizing beta particles for the treatment of various malignant solid tumors.

[212Pb]Pb-ADVC001 is a radioligand therapy comprising Lead-212 conjugated to a Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP)-targeting small-molecule inhibitor, utilizing high-energy alpha particles for the treatment of various advanced malignant solid tumors.

[64Cu]Cu-PSMA-Q is a radiotracer comprising Copper-64 conjugated to a PSMA-targeting ligand (often featuring a "Q" type or similarly optimized scaffold), utilizing positron emission for the PET diagnostic imaging of prostate cancer.

[111In]In-Capromab pendetide is an indium-111 labeled monoclonal antibody targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), used in nuclear medicine imaging for the diagnostic evaluation of prostate cancer in patients post-biopsy or with high risk of recurrence.

[177Lu]Lu-PNT2002 is a lutetium-177 labeled small-molecule radioligand targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), which delivers targeted beta-radiation to induce tumor cell death, used for the treatment of patients with PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) progressing after androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (ARPI) therapy.

[99mTc]Tc-MIP1404 (also known as Technetium Tc 99m trofolastat) is a technetium-99m labeled small-molecule inhibitor targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), used in single-photon emission computed tomography (SPECT/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of primary staging, localization of biochemical recurrence, and companion diagnostic assessment of eligibility for 177Lu-PSMA radiopharmaceutical therapy in patients with prostate cancer.

[99mTc]Tc-EDDA/HYNIC-iPSMA is a technetium-99m labeled small-molecule peptidomimetic inhibitor targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), used in single-photon emission computed tomography (SPECT/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of primary staging, detection of biochemical recurrence, and assessment of metastatic lesions in patients with prostate cancer.

[67Cu]Cu-SAR-bisPSMA is a copper-67 labeled dual-targeted small-molecule radioligand targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), which delivers targeted beta-radiation to induce tumor cell death, used for the treatment of patients with imaging-confirmed PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

[64Cu]Cu-SAR-bisPSMA is a copper-64 labeled dual-targeted small-molecule radioligand targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of lesion screening, staging, and treatment response evaluation in patients with prostate cancer.

[177Lu]Lu-dotadipep is a lutetium-177 labeled small-molecule radioligand targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), which delivers targeted beta-radiation to induce tumor cell death, used for the treatment of patients with PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

[68Ga]Ga-P16-093 is a gallium-68 labeled PSMA-targeting ligand targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA) with a characteristic of low urinary excretion, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT or PET/MRI) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of primary staging, localized lesion detection, and biochemical recurrence in patients with prostate cancer.

[177Lu]Lu-EB-PSMA-617 is a lutetium-177 labeled, Evans Blue (EB)-modified small-molecule radioligand targeting prostate-specific membrane antigen (PSMA), which extends blood circulation half-life and enhances tumor uptake by binding to serum albumin and delivers targeted beta-radiation to induce tumor cell death, used for the treatment of patients with PSMA-positive metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC).

[68Ga]Ga-DOTA.SA.FAPi is a gallium-68 labeled, squaric acid (SA)-modified fibroblast activation protein inhibitor targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) in the tumor microenvironment, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of primary tumors, staging, metastasis detection, and theranostic assessment across various epithelial malignancies (such as breast cancer, sarcoma, gastrointestinal tumors, and high-grade neuroendocrine tumors).

[68Ga]Ga-DATA5m.SA.FAPi is a gallium-68 labeled, squaric acid (SA)-modified fibroblast activation protein inhibitor targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) in the tumor microenvironment, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of various epithelial malignancies (such as NSCLC, pancreatic cancer, prostate cancer, and neuroendocrine tumors) as well as non-malignant fibrotic conditions (such as focal nodular hyperplasia and pulmonary fibrosis).

[177Lu]Lu-vF19 is a lutetium-177 labeled humanized monoclonal antibody targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) in the tumor microenvironment, which delivers targeted beta-radiation to eliminate malignant tumor cells and stromal components, used for the radioimmunotherapy of FAP-positive tumors (such as melanoma).

[68Ga]Ga-FSDD0I is a gallium-68 labeled, albumin-binding fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-targeting ligand, which extends blood circulation half-life and enhances tumor uptake by binding to serum albumin, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT or PET/MRI) for the nuclear medicine companion diagnostic imaging of primary tumors, metastasis detection, and evaluation across various FAP-positive solid tumors (such as hepatocellular carcinoma).

[68Ga]Ga-FSDD1I is a gallium-68 labeled, albumin-binding fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-targeting inhibitor, which extends blood circulation half-life and enhances tumor accumulation by binding to serum albumin, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT or PET/MRI) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging of primary tumors and metastasis detection across various FAP-positive malignancies (such as epithelial malignancies including esophageal and gastric cancers).

[68Ga]Ga-FSDD3I is a gallium-68 labeled, fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-targeted radiopharmaceutical used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT or PET/MRI) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging, primary staging, and metastasis detection across various FAP-positive solid tumors (such as gastrointestinal tumors and lung cancer).

[177Lu]Lu-FSDD0I is a lutetium-177 labeled, albumin-binding fibroblast activation protein (FAP)-targeting ligand, which extends blood circulation half-life and enhances tumor accumulation by binding to serum albumin and delivers targeted beta-radiation to eliminate malignant tumor cells and stromal components, used for the nuclear medicine radionuclide therapy of various FAP-positive solid tumors (such as hepatocellular carcinoma).

[68Ga]Ga-RPS-309 is a gallium-68 labeled, albumin-binding trifunctional small-molecule ligand targeting fibroblast activation protein-alpha (FAP-alpha) in the tumor microenvironment, which extends blood circulation half-life by binding to serum albumin, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging and theranostic assessment of primary tumors and metastasis across various FAP-positive solid tumors (such as liposarcoma).

[68Ga]Ga-DOTAGA-OncoFAP is a gallium-68 labeled high-affinity ultra-small molecule ligand targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP) in the tumor microenvironment, used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT or PET/MRI) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging, primary staging, metastasis screening, and theranostic assessment across various FAP-expressing solid tumors (such as pancreatic cancer, breast cancer, and glioblastoma).

[68Ga]Ga-NODAGA-OncoFAP is a gallium-68 labeled high-affinity ultra-small molecule ligand targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). it is used in PET/CT for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging and theranostic assessment of various FAP-positive solid tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-NOTA-OncoFAP is a gallium-68 labeled high-affinity ultra-small molecule ligand targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). Al[18F]F-NOTA-OncoFAP is an aluminum fluoride-18 labeled high-affinity ultra-small molecule ligand targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It is used in PET/CT for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging and theranostic assessment of various FAP-positive solid tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-TE-FAPI-01 is a lutetium-177 labeled, long-circulating small molecule targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It delivers targeted beta-radiation to eliminate malignant tumor cells and stroma, used for the radionuclide therapy of various FAP-positive solid tumors.

[68Ga]Ga-TE-FAPI-04 is a gallium-68 labeled, structurally optimized long-circulating small molecule targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It is used in positron emission tomography (PET/CT) for the nuclear medicine diagnostic imaging and theranostic assessment of primary tumors and metastases across various FAP-positive solid tumors.

[86Y]Y-TE-FAPI-04 is an yttrium-86 labeled, long-circulating small molecule targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It is used in PET/CT as a companion diagnostic partner for therapeutic radionuclides, enabling nuclear medicine imaging and precise dosimetry assessment of various FAP-positive solid tumors.

[177Lu]Lu-TE-FAPI-04 is a lutetium-177 labeled, long-circulating small molecule targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It delivers targeted beta-radiation to eliminate tumor cells and the microenvironment stroma, used for the radionuclide therapy of various FAP-positive solid tumors, often forming a theranostic pair with [68Ga]/[86Y]-TE-FAPI-04.

[211At]At-FAPI 3 is an astatine-211 labeled small molecule targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It delivers high-energy, short-range alpha particles to precisely destroy tumor stroma and cancer cells, primarily used for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) of various FAP-positive solid tumors.

[211At]At-FAPI 4 is an astatine-211 labeled small molecule targeting fibroblast activation protein (FAP). It delivers high-energy, short-range alpha particles to precisely destroy tumor stroma and cancer cells for targeted alpha therapy (TAT) of various FAP-positive solid tumors, typically offering optimized clearance kinetics compared to FAPI-04.
